element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundational mechanic from predecessor, refined controls
Replaces yarn balls from first game; primary collectible
Increased durability vs. first game's one-hit vulnerability
New weapon mechanic for offensive gameplay variety
Player choice replaces linear progression; enables route optimization
Historical era-based level settings; visual/thematic variety

typical_tension_triggers: Time pressure (implied by theme park urgency)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Moderate; described as 'boringly easy for experienced players' suggesting accessible tension cycles with frequent relief windows

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Multiple paths through each stage enable optional exploration

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible; improved controls over first game, three-hit resilience allows learning
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate difficulty with introduced Ballzooka weapon and themed world variations
notable_spikes: Game perceived as 'boringly easy' overall (GamePro review)

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels set in historical era-based environments within the Amazatorium theme park; provides visual and thematic variety across stage selection
Each stage features multiple traversal paths, allowing players to choose routes and optimize for exploration or speed
Major departure from first game; player selects stages rather than progressing linearly, enabling custom difficulty curves

data_quality_notes: T3 tier (qualitative/empirical); Wikipedia source lacks granular level design documentation. Element introduction order inferred from gameplay mechanics descriptions and story context. Tension/relief rhythm based on reviewer characterizations rather than direct design documentation. Spatial structure inferred from 'multiple paths' and stage selection features mentioned.
